envelope_defaults.human_readable_summary: Dandelion maps the territory. Admins approve the map. Foundation governs what can happen inside the map. DMWs scope what can be remembered. Digital Twins operate within the approved terrain.
envelope_defaults.model_usage_notes: Memory / DMW Map intake establishes the memory-scope ceiling per ADR-0001 + ADR-0080 + RULE 0 sovereignty. Customers should not pay extra just to have memory be safe.

items.name: Memory / DMW Map Assessment
items.description: Stage B intake substrate for the Memory / DMW Map. Maps memory scope per role / user / project / client / team / board / legal / temporary delegation. Establishes which categories belong to self-scoped vs team-scoped vs forbidden DMW scope. Baseline DMW safety is never paywalled.
items.human_readable_summary: Asks what memory belongs where. Outputs DMW scope candidates pending admin + user approval. Baseline DMW safety is in every plan; advanced governance is premium.
items.model_usage_notes: Per RULE 0: entity sovereignty over memory is absolute. Per Wave 2.1 §25 per-role DMW scope notes. Per ADR-0083: DMW baseline included in every plan; advanced governance is a separate pack.
required_questions: What memory is self-scoped (your operating context, never visible to others)?
What memory is team-scoped / hive-scoped (shared within team boundary)?
What memory is client / customer-scoped (customer-by-customer scope binding)?
What memory is board-scoped (read-only / executive summary)?
What memory is legal / compliance-scoped (legal hold / retention)?
optional_questions: What temporary delegated memory exists (PTO coverage / interim role)?
What non-work / private content must be suppressed by construction?
What forgetting / disconnect requirements apply (RULE 0 revocability)?
What safe metadata retention applies (audit chain integrity per ADR-0002)?
What memory review points exist (periodic admin review / regulator review per ADR-0036)?
admin_questions: Which memory categories must the Map propose at first activation?
Which memory categories are forbidden for initial rollout (sensitive / regulated / pre-approval-only)?
Which memory categories require advanced DMW governance pack per ADR-0083?
Which memory categories require ADR-0036 LawfulBasis at regulator-view tier?
Are there any RULE-20-protected forbidden memory categories?
user_questions: Does the Map represent your memory boundaries accurately?
What memory must be private to you only (forbidden to team / org / managers)?
What memory should automatically expire (per-conversation / per-engagement / per-project)?
evidence_sources: Wave 2.1 §25 per-role DMW scope notes at docs/ootb-catalog/role-depth/
ADR-0046 dual-context AI_AGENT routing (Personal AI Agent vs Enterprise AI Agent)
output_candidates: Memory / DMW Map proposal (self / team / project / client / board / legal / temporary scope)
Advanced DMW governance pack requirements list (per ADR-0083)
governance_review_points: RULE 0 sovereignty over entity memory is absolute
Baseline DMW safety is never paywalled per ADR-0083
Customers should not pay extra just to have memory be safe
User must confirm self-scoped memory boundaries
Admin must approve team / org / project / client memory regions
Per ADR-0046 dual-context: Personal AI Agent maps to PERSONAL wallet; Enterprise AI Agent maps to ENTERPRISE wallet
safe_defaults: Baseline DMW safety included regardless of plan
DMW_scope_implications: Memory / DMW Map directly establishes DMW scope boundaries for activation
Forbidden memory categories cannot scope any DMW without explicit later admin authorization
Per-role memory scope from Wave 2.1 §25 constrains per-role DMW activation
Advanced DMW governance pack required for custom retention / regulated deletion variants per ADR-0083
Per ADR-0046: AI_AGENT EntityType maps to PERSONAL or ENTERPRISE wallet by deployment context
audit_expectations: existing DMW capsule audit literals preserved
no new audit literal at D2
